starting_regions.name: The Thornsveld Marches
starting_regions.danger_notes: Escalates quickly if involved in mine situation, succession tension, or unprepared wilderness travel

geographic_character.terrain: Dark conifer forest broken by granite ridgelines and river valleys
geographic_character.climate: Short violent summers, long lethal winters. Snow seasons. Light barely clears treeline in winter.
geographic_character.smells: Pine resin, wet stone, woodsmoke, animal dung near settlements
geographic_character.sounds: Wind, wolves, distant rivers. Settlements are quiet and watchful.
geographic_character.feel: Everything pushes to complete its cycle before the cold returns. The forest smells of time. Hospitality is genuine and near-mandatory — you feed a traveler because next winter it might be you.
geographic_character.wildlife: Wolves, bears, unnamed things in the deep forest that locals do not name directly

primary.name: Durnward
primary.type: River town — largest in the Marches

political_condition.formal_authority: Thornsveld Crown Administration via Lady Esven Cravenmoor (Marchwarden)
political_condition.contested_by: The Karn Clans (high ground and forest passes)

marchwarden.name: Lady Esven Cravenmoor
marchwarden.description: Competent, pragmatic, quietly terrified of the coming succession. Negotiating with the Karn Clans in secret.
marchwarden.secret: Conducting unauthorized negotiations with Karn Clan-Chief Halvard Ironmark. Aldric IV unofficially tolerates this.

king.name: Aldric IV
king.situation: No legitimate heir. Three nephews positioning: Corvan (eldest, military), Pelvar (Bent Loom Guild-backed), Jorn (young, popular with common people — makes him dangerous)
king.prognosis: Will not live another two years. Everyone knows.

economic_state.currency_level: Iron chits at village level, bronze marks at market. Steel discs rare.

crisis_point.name: The Durnwald Mine
crisis_point.situation: Partial collapse 8 months ago. 41 miners dead. Structurally recoverable but no workers will return. Compensation to families not yet paid.

factions.name: Thornsveld Crown Administration
factions.role: Legitimate authority, stretched thin

factions.name: The Karn Clans
factions.role: Not legally present in lowlands — practically everywhere. Clan traders at market, hunters in forest.

factions.name: The Undying Ember
factions.role: One temple in Durnward. Prior Brother Soren Aldrath — devout and meddlesome, has opinions about Karn negotiations.

factions.name: The Bent Loom Guild
factions.role: Controls most legitimate commerce. Publicly neutral. Secretly backing nephew Pelvar financially.

factions.name: Informal armed individuals
factions.role: The Marchwarden hires individuals rather than mercenary companies — cheaper and less legally complicated.

other_religions: Karn Old Ways (high ground and deep forest)
starting_regions.ambient_tension: Aldric IV's imminent death. Three nephews in position. The Marchwarden's secret Karn negotiations could stabilize the region or, at the wrong moment, be used as a treason charge. The mine families want their money.
starting_hooks: Levy soldier: Recently released from a garrison whose budget was just cut. Owed two months back pay in iron chits that may or may not arrive.
Tradesperson: Arrived in Durnward looking for work. Smithy on east road needs a smith. Bent Loom Guild warehouse hiring. Marchwarden's hall needs a carpenter.
Nobody: Walked out of the forest three days ago with nothing. Temple will feed you once. After that — work or leave.

starting_regions.name: The Keth Plateau

geographic_character.terrain: High dry plateau, ~1,400 feet elevation. Pale dust and grey scrub. Dry riverbeds that flood violently and briefly during seasonal rains.
geographic_character.climate: Thin air cold at night even when days are burning. The sky is enormous. Horizon always visible.
geographic_character.distances: Deceptive — what looks like a two-hour walk is often half a day
geographic_character.ruins: Everywhere. Normalized. Children play in them. Scholars from other continents find this deeply distressing.
geographic_character.nights: Extraordinary — no light pollution, high altitude, Milky Way visible as a physical thing
geographic_character.feel: A loneliness that gets into people. Immense and old and indifferent.

primary.name: Keth
primary.type: Major city — trade crossroads and ruin-salvage hub
features: The Archive of Keth (3,000 years old)

political_condition.formal_authority: The Dust Council — seven seats held by the seven wealthiest trading houses. Composition shifts with wealth rankings.
political_condition.outside_city: The Salvage Authority — licenses ruin excavation, taxes extraction, maintains road-guard force. Control fades to nothing beyond 30 miles.
political_condition.deep_plateau: Drift Lords do whatever they want. Authority officially ignores this.
political_condition.current_crisis: Two of seven Council seats disputed — one house in succession crisis, one under currency manipulation investigation. Solvane merchant consortium attempting to buy out a third house.

pillars: Trade routing (intersection of two major cross-continental roads)
Ruin salvage (Varathi materials command extraordinary prices)
Water (plateau aquifer — the only reliable water for hundreds of miles — Keth charges for access)
economic_state.scarcity: Food. Almost nothing grows on the plateau. Everything edible comes in by caravan from Solvane.
economic_state.vulnerability: A bad Solvane harvest season triggers Keth food crisis within 60 days. Has happened three times in living memory.
economic_state.current_threat: Solvane merchant consortium attempting to control both Keth's food supply and a Council seat simultaneously.

factions.name: The Dust Council
factions.role: Formal city authority

factions.name: The Salvage Authority
factions.role: Formal outside authority — limited range

factions.name: The First Scholars / Archive of Keth
factions.role: Moral authority. Most significant temple complex in the known world. Best-informed institution on the continent.

factions.name: The Varathi Return
factions.role: Illegal in Keth. Present anyway through salvage networks. Extremely interested in recent discoveries.

factions.name: The Halverness Factors
factions.role: Major Cauldenmoor trading house. Trying to broker the Solvane consortium situation for a fee.

factions.name: The Drift Lords
factions.role: Present in market district as traders. Moving slightly differently than usual.

starting_regions.ambient_tension: Six weeks ago a salvage crew found something in a deep-plateau ruin site. The crew has been absorbed into the Archive for 'protective consultation' — they are not leaving. The Varathi Return cult is extremely interested. Two Council houses are extremely interested. The Drift Lords are moving differently.
starting_regions.macro_thread: The thing found in the Ossrath ruins connects to the ship from Ossrath in the Shattered Reach Anchorage — the first thread of the current era's deep macro-tension.
starting_hooks: Caravan guard or traveling tradesperson: The city is humming with a specific quality of tension — people are talking about something without saying what it is.
Scholar's assistant: The Archive is hiring for a cataloguing expedition.
Salvage worker: Two Council houses are quietly hiring individuals for unspecified plateau tasks.
Road-guard candidate: The Salvage Authority needs guards for a three-week circuit patrol.

starting_regions.name: The Amber Coast

geographic_character.terrain: White stone cliffs, sheltered harbors, olive groves on hillsides, fishing villages in every cove
geographic_character.climate: Warm, humid, intensely alive. Nearly year-round growing season.
geographic_character.color: The sea turns amber-gold in late afternoon — miles of it along the coast
geographic_character.smell: Salt, fish, olive oil, spice in market districts. Salt, fish, damp, sewage in poorer quarters.
geographic_character.poverty_character: Starvation here is about coin and access, not subsistence. A starving person on the Amber Coast is being squeezed by an economic system, not failing against nature.
geographic_character.wealth_gap: Enormous and visible. Marble-fronted merchant houses three streets from one-room tenement warrens.

settlements.primary: Multiple coastal cities — white-walled, terraced up cliffs
settlements.secondary: Fishing villages in every cove, coastal road towns

political_condition.formal_authority: The Factorate of the Coast — appointed body managing trade licensing, port taxation, road maintenance
political_condition.factor_general: Appointed by Queen Aldeth, personally loyal to her, uncertain about what happens next

queen.name: Aldeth Calmaveth III
queen.assessment: Most politically sophisticated ruler currently living
queen.secret: Seriously ill. Has not made this public. Court has noticed. Other powers have heard rumors.
queen.heir: Prince Pelvan, 23, untested, being advised by three different factions simultaneously

economic_state.currency_level: Steel discs are normal city currency. Iron chits are almost insultingly small denomination.
economic_state.stress: Solvanic Dominion has been overextending trade credit to Keth and two Cauldenmoor powers simultaneously. If any destabilize, Dominion finances get complicated. Not public knowledge.

factions.name: The Factorate of the Coast
factions.role: Effective civic authority

factions.name: The Tidewarden Church
factions.role: Dominant religion, wealthy, owns ~15% of coastal property

factions.name: The Guild of the Sealed Ledger
factions.role: Notary and banking. Holds debt records on half the coast. Neutral in all conflicts — neutrality is their value proposition.

factions.name: The Halverness Factors
factions.role: Major trading presence — their home continent

factions.name: The Salt Silence
factions.role: Small, unsettling, present in every port city

factions.name: Corsair Throne agents
factions.role: Present in every major port ensuring the non-aggression payment flows, occasionally doing other things

other_religions: Salt Silence (present but not quite a religion in the conventional sense)
starting_regions.ambient_tension: Queen Aldeth's illness. Succession uncertainty. Trading houses repositioning. The Tidewarden Church calmly preparing to outlast another ruler. The Corsair Throne watching whether the non-aggression arrangement survives transition. A place that is beautiful, functional, wealthy — and holding its breath.
starting_hooks: Dock worker or ship's crew: Most options available. The harbor is hiring.
Literate and presentable: Guild of the Sealed Ledger will hire as a copying clerk within a day.
Tradesperson with skill: Guild workshops are accessible.
Nothing at all: Dock labor and a shared sleeping room for two iron chits a week.

starting_regions.name: The Ironmark Borderlands
starting_regions.danger_notes: Most dangerous starting region. Also most economically rewarding for capable characters. Bandits are organized. Political violence is semi-regular. Legal protection is inconsistent.

geographic_character.terrain: Fertile river valleys between low forested hills. Navigable waterways. Arable land suppressed by instability.
geographic_character.history_visible: Burned villages with one or two rebuilt buildings. Fields showing furrow patterns of older agricultural systems. Cannibal ized Hegemony road paving. Watchtowers on every ridge — some manned, some not.
geographic_character.smell: Turned earth and, on the wrong wind, old smoke
geographic_character.people: Particular watchfulness — the kind that comes from living in a place where things change violently without warning

primary_settlement.name: Aldenmere's Cross
primary_settlement.type: Semi-fortified market town — informal regional capital

political_condition.formal_authority: Kingdom of Durn via Lord-Marshal Halvard Brennwatch

lord_marshal.name: Halvard Brennwatch
lord_marshal.description: Not popular. Respected. Has held this region together through sheer competence for 30 years.
lord_marshal.crisis: Has been requesting reinforcements from Durn's capital for 4 months. Has received promises instead of soldiers.

ironmark_pressure.method: Expanding westward 6 years through local negotiation, strategic bribery, and swift military action presented as policing. Has taken 3 settlements.
ironmark_pressure.next_target: Cravenmoor's Ford — sits on the region's most important river crossing. Taking it severs Durn's eastern garrison resupply.

political_condition.legal_reality: Three different legal systems operating simultaneously depending on which village you're in and who you ask.

economic_state.reality: Suppressed by instability. Farmers plow fields they're not sure they'll harvest.
economic_state.opportunity: Security is scarce and people will pay for it. Fighting characters have obvious immediate economic value here.

factions.name: Durn Crown Administration
factions.role: Represented by Brennwatch and thinning garrison commanders

factions.name: The Ironmark
factions.role: Formally absent from Durn territory. Practically present through agents, bribed officials, and three controlled settlements.

factions.name: The Grey Contracts
factions.role: Mid-sized mercenary company (~400 members). Contracted to Brennwatch for border patrol. Captain Vael Sorn — keeps contracts to the letter and not a word further.

factions.name: The Reclaimed Path
factions.role: Dominant religion in Durn-administered territory

factions.name: The Scorched Covenant
factions.role: Dominant in Ironmark territories — legitimizing Brekhard's expansion

factions.name: The Hollow Road
factions.role: Criminal information network in no-man's-land settlements. Contraband, information, unofficial banking.

starting_regions.dominant_religion: The Reclaimed Path (Durn) / The Scorched Covenant (Ironmark) — divided along political fault line
other_religions: Old Ways (persisting stubbornly in oldest villages)
Barefoot Compact (itinerant preachers, neither side controls them)
starting_regions.ambient_tension: Brekhard is about to move on Cravenmoor's Ford. Brennwatch knows. Capital won't send soldiers. Grey Contracts watching their contract carefully — they may be asked to do something they weren't hired for.
starting_hooks: Grey Contracts recruiting notice: Up in Aldenmere's Cross.
Brennwatch garrison: Hiring auxiliaries for border patrol.
Merchant escort: Three merchants need caravan guards for a run east.
Honest labor: Farmer outside town needs harvest help — two workers left for the city and haven't returned.
Escort mission: Reclaimed Path priest needs escort to a village 3 days north where his predecessor just died under unclear circumstances.

starting_regions.name: The Free Fens
starting_regions.danger_level: Moderate (unusual character — environmental danger high, human danger relatively low)
starting_regions.danger_notes: Knowing what you're doing here is everything. Characters without bushcraft/survival skills are in genuine danger from terrain. Navigation error = drowning in something that looks like solid ground.

geographic_character.terrain: Several hundred miles of fen, bog, dark forest, moor. Water table near surface everywhere. Roads are boardwalks. Buildings on stilts or raised earthen platforms.
geographic_character.forest: Old in a way other continents' forests aren't. Trees from Year 0 still standing. Close and tall, canopy shuts out most light. Midday looks like dusk.
geographic_character.sound: Carries strangely — sometimes further than should be possible, sometimes disappears completely.
geographic_character.smell: Peat, standing water, in settlements: smoke and wet wool
geographic_character.feel: Isolated. The world's largest unbroken wilderness on Arneth. Dark and ancient and patient.

primary_settlement.name: Mossward
primary_settlement.type: Largest Fens settlement — informal capital, market town

political_condition.practical_governance: ~30 semi-independent village councils, 3 larger towns, and the Fen Lords (informal rotating council of most powerful village heads, meets twice yearly at Mossward)
political_condition.drenmari_see: Closest thing to governance — network of temple-Stations serving as waypoints, shelters, community centers, and de facto courts. Authority is moral and logistical, not military.
political_condition.ashenveld_claim: The Ashenveld Monarchy nominally claims the Fens. This claim is ignored by everyone in the Fens and weakly pressed by Ashenveld. A census-taker is sent every 5 years, gets lost, returns with incomplete data.

exports: Medicinal plants (several species found nowhere else, high prices)
imports: Grain (via Mossward market — almost nothing grows in the Fens)
economic_state.currency_level: Substantially non-monetary at village level — barter, labor exchange, community obligation. Market towns use iron chits and bronze marks. Steel discs exist but cause comment.

factions.name: The Drenmari See
factions.role: Moral authority, information network, genuine community service through Station network

factions.name: The Moor Communion
factions.role: Dominant faith and See's institutional expression

factions.name: Fen Lords informal council
factions.role: Practical authority in major collective decisions

factions.name: The Barefoot Compact
factions.role: Significant wandering presence — more welcomed here than anywhere else

factions.name: The Hollow Road
factions.role: Uses the Fens as a route for moving goods and people other powers don't want moved

factions.name: Ashenveld agents
factions.role: Occasional, rarely effective, usually spotted immediately — they don't know how to move through fen terrain

other_religions: Old Ways (the Moor Communion basically is a formalized Old Ways)
starting_regions.ambient_tension: Something is dying in the deep fen. A stretch of peat bog 3 days northwest of Mossward shows unusual die-off — plants dying in a rough 2-mile circle. Water that smells wrong. Three people sick with something the Station's medical practitioners haven't seen before. Could be natural. Could be contamination from deep Varathi ruins in the fen (mostly unknown — deep fen has never been fully surveyed). The See is investigating. Nobody is panicking yet, which in fen culture means it is being taken seriously.
starting_hooks: Trader on the back-road: Using the Fens' route to avoid road taxes or wanted notices elsewhere.
Moor Communion pilgrim: Traveling between Stations.
Hollow Road contact: Arrived through the network, laying low.
Supply run: The See needs someone to make a supply run to the affected Station in the northwest. Everyone local is declining.

starting_regions.name: The Tidewarden Anchorage

geographic_character.terrain: Natural harbor formed by three large islands in a rough triangle. Sheltered deep-water bay ~40 miles across. Rocky, steep islands with salt-adapted scrub.
geographic_character.settlements: Built into cliff faces and down to waterline. Buildings stacked on buildings. Narrow stairs cut into stone. Rooftop gardens. Everything oriented toward the harbor.
geographic_character.light: Far enough south for extraordinary sky — very clear, very blue, fast-moving dramatic clouds. Milky Way visible.
geographic_character.nights: Harbor glows faintly with bioluminescent organisms. Sailors navigate by it.
geographic_character.smell: Tar, salt, fish guts in working areas. Charcoal and spiced meat in better quarters.
geographic_character.sound: Constant — rigging creak, harbor calls, multilingual noise of a major trade hub.
geographic_character.feel: The world in miniature. Every language, every faith, every trade. Lonely in the way that enormous human density is lonely — surrounded by everyone, known by no one.

political_condition.formal_authority: The Reach Compact — council of seven largest island-states
political_condition.practical_authority: The Corsair Throne — controls enough sea lanes to veto Compact decisions

iron_wake.name: Rael Sorveth
iron_wake.title: The Iron Wake
iron_wake.history: Former Reach Compact naval officer. Took the Corsair Throne by force 12 years ago.
iron_wake.current_goal: Formal recognition as legitimate power within Compact structure
iron_wake.strategy: Refusing formal recognition while cooperating practically — gives operational flexibility without accountability

political_condition.mediator: The Tidewarden Church — trying to mediate. Needs both sides functional for its own financial interests.

economic_state.type: Transit economy — produces little, facilitates movement of enormous value
economic_state.banking: Tidewarden Church runs primary banking facility
economic_state.stress: Corsair Throne imposing unofficial tariffs on Cauldenmoor-bound shipping. Cauldenmoor merchants furious. Compact uninvolved officially. Church eating cost differences for important clients.

factions.name: The Reach Compact Council
factions.role: Formal island authority

factions.name: The Corsair Throne
factions.role: Practical sea authority and shadow economy

factions.name: The Tidewarden Church
factions.role: Institutional presence, banking, the cathedral complex

factions.name: The Salt Silence
factions.role: Extremely present, practically invisible. Can tell you the contents of any sealed cargo manifest for a price.

factions.name: The Halverness Factors
factions.role: Regional office, major shipper

factions.name: Foreign trade representatives
factions.role: ~20 of 42 powers have offices or agents here — highest information density of any starting region

other_religions: Every other faith represented somewhere in the foreign quarter
starting_regions.ambient_tension: A ship arrived 3 weeks ago from Ossrath's eastern coast (unusual routing). Its manifest doesn't match its cargo. The harbor inspector was told to file it as a clerical error. The ship remains anchored in the outer harbor — crew hasn't come ashore except for provisioning. The Salt Silence is interested. Two Compact council members are interested. The Tidewarden Church banking office had an unusual visitor the day after the ship arrived.
starting_regions.macro_thread: This ship's cargo connects to the thing found in the Ossrath ruins (Keth Plateau region). A character starting in Keth and a character starting in the Anchorage are 3,000 miles apart and pulling on threads of the same knot.
starting_hooks: Arriving by ship: Standard entry. The harbor is hiring for everything.
Dock labor or warehouse: Immediately available.
Corsair Throne office (second island): Hiring for unspecified maritime work, no background questions.
Salt Silence contact: They can be found if you know where to look. They are always interested in new information sources.
Church administrative work: Available for literate characters.
